seoTitle: Claremont vs Clarence Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Claremont and Clarence Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Claremont vs Clarence Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Claremont and Clarence Point.
heading: Claremont vs Clarence Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Claremont, Tasmania and Clarence Point, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clarence Point has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Claremont (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Claremont or Clarence Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Claremont has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Clarence Point scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Claremont scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Clarence Point scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Claremont or Clarence Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clarence Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.77% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
